days.title: Rooms of the home: where I live

grammar.title: Present tense of жить + 'в' for location
grammar.explanation: Today we **review the present tense** with the verb **жить** (to live), which is slightly irregular: я живу́, ты живёшь, он/она́ живёт, мы живём, вы живёте, они́ живу́т (note the inserted **в**). To say *where* someone lives, use the preposition **в** (in) + the **prepositional case**. For most feminine nouns ending in -а, change -а to **-е**: кварти́р**а** → в кварти́р**е**.

examples.english: I live in an apartment.
Do you live here?
They live in a big house.

reading.title: Где ты живёшь?
reading.text: — Где ты живёшь? — Я живу здесь, в квартире. Это большой дом.
reading.translation: — Where do you live? — I live here, in an apartment. It is a big house.

days.speakingPrompt: Say aloud where you live: 'Я живу в...' and describe whether it is a house or an apartment, and whether it is big. Use жить in the correct я-form.

days.title: Furniture and the prepositional case 'в комнате'

vocab.exampleEn: There is a bed in the room.
The book is on the table.

grammar.title: Prepositional case with в for masculine and feminine nouns
grammar.explanation: We continue the **prepositional case** for location. The ending **-е** applies to most masculine and feminine nouns: стол → в стол**е**, ко́мната → в ко́мнат**е**. Use the verb **стоя́ть** (to stand) to say where furniture *is located*: оно́ стои́т, они́ стоя́т. Remember **в** + prepositional answers the question **где?** (where?).

examples.english: The chair is (stands) in the room.
There is a new bed in the room.
The wardrobe stands there.

reading.title: В моей комнате
reading.text: В комнате стоит кровать. Здесь новый стол и стул. Шкаф стоит там.
reading.translation: In the room there is a bed. Here is a new table and a chair. The wardrobe stands there.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe your own room: name three pieces of furniture and say where each one is using 'в комнате', 'здесь', or 'там' with стоит/стоят.

days.title: Doors, windows and 'на' for surfaces

vocab.exampleEn: The picture is on the wall.
The cat is on the floor.
The book is lying on the table.
The picture hangs on the wall.

grammar.title: The preposition на + prepositional case
grammar.explanation: Like **в**, the preposition **на** (on/at) takes the **prepositional case** and answers **где?**. Use **на** for surfaces: на стол**е** (on the table), на стен**е** (on the wall), на пол**у** (on the floor — note the special ending **-у**). Pair it with location verbs: **лежа́ть** (to lie) for flat objects, **висе́ть** (to hang) for things on walls.

examples.english: The book is lying on the table.
The picture hangs on the wall.
The cat is lying on the floor.

reading.title: Светлая комната
reading.text: Это светлая комната. Окно большое. На стене висит картина, а книга лежит на столе.
reading.translation: This is a bright room. The window is big. A picture hangs on the wall, and a book is lying on the table.

days.speakingPrompt: Look around your room. Say what is on the table, on the wall, and on the floor using на + prepositional case with лежит or висит.

days.title: Who does what at home: present tense practice

vocab.exampleEn: I am sitting on the chair.
The cat sleeps on the bed.
Mum is reading in the room.

grammar.title: Present tense conjugation review with home verbs
grammar.explanation: Review the two present-tense patterns. **First conjugation** (-ть → -ю/-ешь/-ет...): чита́ть → я чита́**ю**, ты чита́**ешь**, он чита́**ет**. **Second conjugation** (-ить/-еть → -ю/-ишь/-ит...): сиде́ть → я сижу́, ты сиди́**шь**, он сиди́**т** (note ж in the я-form). Combine actions with location: **в/на** + prepositional tells **где** the action happens. The word **дома** means *at home* and never changes.

examples.english: I am reading in the room.
The cat sleeps on the bed.
We are resting at home now.

reading.title: Дома
reading.text: Папа сейчас дома. Он сидит на стуле и читает. Мама тоже дома, она отдыхает в комнате.
reading.translation: Dad is at home now. He is sitting on the chair and reading. Mum is also at home; she is resting in the room.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe what the people in your home are doing right now: use сейчас, дома, and at least two verbs (читать, сидеть, спать, отдыхать) with в/на + prepositional.

days.title: Talking about your kitchen: questions with где

vocab.exampleEn: The refrigerator is in the kitchen.
The dishes are on the table.
Mum is cooking in the kitchen.

grammar.title: Asking and answering где? with в/на
grammar.explanation: Put it all together by asking **Где...?** (Where...?) and answering with **в** or **на** + prepositional case. Use **в** for enclosed spaces (в ко́мнат**е**, в дом**е**) and **на** for open areas and certain words like **ку́хня** → на ку́хн**е**. Notice that ку́хня ends in -я, so the prepositional is **-е**. The verb **гото́вить** (2nd conj.) keeps its stem: я гото́влю, ты гото́вишь.

examples.english: — Where is Mum? — In the kitchen.
Mum is cooking in the kitchen.
The dishes are in the cupboard.

reading.title: На кухне
reading.text: — Где мама? — Она на кухне, она готовит. Кухня чистая и уютная.
reading.translation: — Where is Mum? — She is in the kitchen, she is cooking. The kitchen is clean and cosy.

days.speakingPrompt: Ask and answer 'Где...?' about three things in a kitchen (холодильник, плита, посуда). Answer with на кухне / в шкафу and add an adjective like чистый or уютный.

days.title: Describing your whole home (putting it together)

vocab.exampleEn: The door is in the corridor.
There is a carpet on the floor.
The carpet is on the floor.

grammar.title: Combining present tense + prepositional in connected speech
grammar.explanation: Today you combine everything: describe a home using several **в/на + prepositional** phrases and **present-tense verbs** in one connected text. Remember the key location endings: **-е** for most nouns (в дом**е**, на стен**е**), and the special **-у** for пол → на пол**у** and шкаф → в шкаф**у**. Adjectives agree with their noun: ма́леньк**ая** ко́мната, краси́в**ый** дом.

examples.english: There is a wardrobe in the corridor.
A beautiful carpet lies on the floor.
Our house is small but cosy.

reading.title: Наш дом
reading.text: Это наш дом. Он маленький, но красивый. В коридоре стоит шкаф, а на полу лежит ковёр.
reading.translation: This is our house. It is small but beautiful. In the corridor there is a wardrobe, and on the floor lies a carpet.

days.speakingPrompt: Give a short tour of your home in 4–5 sentences. Name at least three rooms and say what stands or lies in each, using в/на + prepositional and adjectives that agree.

days.title: Week 8 review: home, furniture and location

grammar.title: Recap: present tense + prepositional case (где?)
grammar.explanation: This week consolidated two core tools. **Present tense**: 1st conjugation (чита́ю, чита́ешь) and 2nd conjugation (сижу́, сиди́шь), plus the verbs жить, стоя́ть, лежа́ть, висе́ть, сиде́ть, спать, гото́вить. **Prepositional case** answers **где?**: use **в** for enclosed spaces and **на** for surfaces/open areas, with the ending **-е** for most nouns and the special **-у** in на полу́ / в шкафу́. Adjectives agree in gender with their noun.

examples.english: The chair is in the room.
The picture hangs on the wall.
Mum is cooking in the kitchen.

reading.title: Дома вечером
reading.text: — Где ты сейчас? — Я дома, в комнате. Я сижу на стуле и читаю. — А где мама? — Она на кухне, готовит. Наш дом маленький, но уютный.
reading.translation: — Where are you now? — I am at home, in the room. I am sitting on the chair and reading. — And where is Mum? — She is in the kitchen, cooking. Our house is small but cosy.

days.speakingPrompt: Without notes, describe where you are and what you are doing right now, then describe one other room and who is there. Use at least four в/на + prepositional phrases and three different present-tense verbs.
